author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Written in the form of a letter addressed to the Catholics, in which the first portion of the letter which Petilian had written to his adherents is examined and refuted.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Lastly, it has been said, as he himself has also quoted, "Ye shall know them by their fruits:" let us therefore examine into their fruits.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: You bring up against our predecessors their delivery of the sacred books.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: This very charge we urge with greater probability against their accusers themselves.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And not to carry our search too far, in the same city of Constantina your predecessors ordained Silvanus bishop at the very outset of his schism.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: He, while he was still a subdeacon, was most unmistakeably entered as a traditor in the archives of the city.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If you on your side bring forward documents against our predecessors, all that we ask is equal terms, that we should either believe both to be true or both to be false.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If both are true, you are unquestionably guilty of schism, who have pretended that you avoid offenses in the communion of the whole world, which you had commonly among you in the small fragment of your own sect.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But again, if both are false, you are unquestionably guilty of schism, who, on account of the false charges of giving up the sacred books, are staining yourselves with the heinous offence of severance from the Church.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But if we have something to urge in accusation while you have nothing, or if our charges are true whilst yours are false, it is no longer matter of discussion how thoroughly your mouths are closed.
